classData.description: A unique, non-enumerable key for the internal event map. Using a Symbol prevents property name collisions on the consuming class instance, providing a robust way to manage private state within a mixin.
classData.name: eventMapSymbol

classData.comment:
/**
         * A declarative way to assign event listeners to an instance upon creation.
         * The framework processes this config and calls `on()` to populate the
         * internal event registry. This config should not be manipulated directly after
         * instantiation; use `on()` and `un()` instead.
         * @member {Object|null} listeners_
         * @example
         * listeners: {
         *     myEvent: 'onMyEvent',
         *     otherEvent: {
         *         fn: 'onOtherEvent',
         *         delay: 100,
         *         once: true
         *     },
         *     scope: this
         * }
         * @reactive
         */
classData.description: A declarative way to assign event listeners to an instance upon creation. The framework processes this config and calls on() to populate the internal event registry. This config should not be manipulated directly after instantiation; use on() and un() instead.
classData.name: listeners_

classData.comment:
/**
     * @param {Object|String} name
     * @param {Object} [opts]
     * @param {Object} [scope]
     * @param {String} [eventId]
     * @param {Object} [data]
     * @param {Number|String} [order]
     * @returns {String|null} eventId null in case an object gets passed as the name (multiple ids)
     */

returns.description: eventId null in case an object gets passed as the name (multiple ids)

classData.name: addListener

classData.comment:
/**
     * This hook is the bridge between the declarative `listeners_` config and the
     * imperative `on()`/`un()` methods. It's called automatically by the framework
     * whenever the `listeners` config property is changed.
     * @param {Object} value The new listeners object
     * @param {Object} oldValue The old listeners object
     * @protected
     */
classData.description: This hook is the bridge between the declarative listeners_ config and the imperative on()/un() methods. It's called automatically by the framework whenever the listeners config property is changed.
classData.name: afterSetListeners

classData.comment:
/**
     * Call the passed function, or a function by *name* which exists in the passed scope's
     * or this component's ownership chain.
     * @param {Function|String} fn A function, or the name of a function to find in the passed scope object.
     * @param {Object} scope       The scope to find the function in if it is specified as a string.
     * @param {Array} args         Arguments to pass to the callback.
     */
classData.description: Call the passed function, or a function by name which exists in the passed scope's or this component's ownership chain.

params.description: A function, or the name of a function to find in the passed scope object.
params.name: fn

params.description: The scope to find the function in if it is specified as a string.
params.name: scope

classData.name: callback

     * @param {Object} cb
     * @param {Array} args
     * @param {Number} delay
     */
classData.description: Internal helper method for events which use the delay option
classData.name: delayedCallback

classData.comment:
/**
     * @summary Remove listeners by id, handler, or object-valued listener specs.
     *
     * There are different syntax's how you can use this method.
     * Using the eventId:
     * ```
     * this.removeListener('change', 'neo-event-7');
     * ```
     * Passing the handler method:
     * ```
     * this.removeListener('change', this.onChange, this);
     * ```
     * Passing an object:
     * ```
     * me.field.un({
     *     change                    : me.onFieldChange,
     *     changeClearToOriginalValue: me.onFieldChange,
     *     scope                     : me
     * });
     * ```
     * @param {Object|String} name
     * @param {Function|String} [eventId]
     * @param {Neo.core.Base} [scope]
     */
classData.summary:
Remove listeners by id, handler, or object-valued listener specs.

There are different syntax's how you can use this method.
Using the eventId:
```
this.removeListener('change', 'neo-event-7');
```
Passing the handler method:
```
this.removeListener('change', this.onChange, this);
```
Passing an object:
```
me.field.un({
    change                    : me.onFieldChange,
    changeClearToOriginalValue: me.onFieldChange,
    scope                     : me
});
```
classData.name: removeListener

classData.description: Serializes the observable state into a JSON-compatible object.
classData.name: toJSON
